payload.answer: Rejected. The proposal attempts to convert a large tabular dataset into 500 durable memory cards, one per CSV row. This is not an appropriate use of durable personal memory because it atomizes bulk data without demonstrating long-term relevance, salience, or consolidation. Committing row-level entries at this scale would create noise, reduce retrieval quality, and risk preserving transient or low-value facts as if they were important personal memories. A validator should require summarization, selective extraction of stable high-signal facts, or storage in an external structured system rather than Brain memory cards.

entity_resolution.reason: No entity resolution should occur because the proposed write is rejected before ingestion.

payload.intent: Evaluate and critique a proposed memory write that atomizes a large CSV into 500 per-row durable memory cards.

details: Rejected bulk memory write of 500 proposed cards.
Detected excessive atomization of tabular data into durable memory.
Recommended summarization or external structured storage instead of row-level ingestion.

repair_options: Do not create one memory card per CSV row.
Summarize the table at a higher level, capturing only enduring and behaviorally relevant patterns.
Extract only a small number of high-salience facts that are likely to matter in future interactions.
Store the full CSV in an external document/database and save at most a pointer or summary in memory.
If row-level facts are needed, gate them behind explicit criteria such as long-term importance, user preference, or repeated future utility.
Group related rows into aggregate memories instead of atomized per-record cards.
